text:
**Dispatch path.** Management/"mgmt" commands are ordinary kind:9 chat messages p‑tagging the daemon's backend key. `src/daemon/server/management_command.rs:24` — `is_management_command_for_backend()` detects any `KIND_CHAT` event that p‑tags `state.backend_pubkey()`. `handle_management_command()` (line 34) claims the command idempotently, calls `execute_claimed()` → `parse_command()` (`src/daemon/server/management_command/parse.rs`), which recognizes `list agents`, `list sessions`, `add agent`, `kill`, `archive`. `list agents` is handled by `src/daemon/server/management_command/list_agents.rs:8`, reading the local agent roster and formatting a `"mgmt ok: N agent(s) on <host>"` string.

**Reply is a real broadcast chat event, not private.** `publish_reply()` (`src/daemon/server/management_command.rs:199-224`) builds a normal `ChatMessage` from `state.host` (rendered as `"laptop (tenex-edge)"`) and calls `provider.publish_chat_checked(&chat, &keys, &record)`. That flows into `src/fabric/provider/chat.rs:45-93`: `publish_chat_checked` → `sign_chat_message` (signs a genuine **kind:9** nostr event via `self.wire.encode(&DomainEvent::ChatMessage(...))`) → `publish_signed_chat_event_checked` → `self.transport.publish_event_checked(signed)` — i.e. it is actually published to the relay into the same NIP‑29 group (`channel_h`), exactly like any agent's chat message. It is then also mirrored locally via `seed_chat_read_models()` into the `relay_events` and `messages` SQLite tables. [...] **2. Filtering logic exists — but only in `src/fabric_context/` (the hook/awareness snapshot path).**
- `src/fabric_context/messages.rs:158-190`: `is_backend_traffic()` excludes any chat event whose author OR any p-tag recipient is either `input.backend_pubkey` (the daemon's own mgmt key) or a pubkey whose cached kind:0 sets `is_backend` (via `is_backend_pubkey`/`is_backend` helpers, `p.is_backend`). [...] **2. Mgmt/backend exchanges leaking into shared channel history**
`handle_chat_read` now filters out backend-authored or backend-p-tagged rows (both in the initial batch and the `--live` tail), reusing the exact `is_backend_pubkey` check that already protected the hook/awareness snapshot (`fabric_context::messages::is_backend_traffic`) — just bumped to `pub(crate)` and applied here too, so `list agents`-style mgmt round-trips no longer show up when another agent reads the channel.
